title: APUSH LEQ Spanish-American War turning point
prompt: Evaluate the extent to which the Spanish-American War marked a turning point in American foreign policy.

terms.definition: Evaluate the extent to which the Spanish-American War marked a turning point in American foreign policy.
The Spanish-American War of 1898 marked a decisive shift in American foreign policy from continental expansion toward overseas imperialism and global intervention.
Before 1898, the United States largely pursued isolationism, emphasizing westward expansion and avoiding permanent alliances in European affairs as seen in the Monroe Doctrine.
Victory in the Spanish-American War brought the Philippines, Guam, and Puerto Rico under American control, signaling a break from earlier anti-imperial traditions.
The Open Door Notes and Roosevelt Corollary after 1898 show the United States asserting power beyond the Western Hemisphere, confirming a new imperial posture.
Although earlier expansion across North America continued older patterns, the Spanish-American War was a turning point because it committed the United States to sustained overseas empire.
